heading: Tax imposed on certain built-in gains
label: 1374 Tax imposed on certain built-in gains

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: If for any taxable year beginning in the recognition period an S corporation has a net recognized built-in gain, there is hereby imposed a tax (computed under subsection (b)) on the income of such corporation for such taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of the tax imposed by subsection (a) shall be computed by applying the highest rate of tax specified in section 11(b) to the net recognized built-in gain of the S corporation for the taxable year.

children.heading: Net operating loss carryforwards from C years allowed
children.label: 2 Net operating loss carryforwards from C years allowed
children.text: Notwithstanding section 1371(b)(1), any net operating loss carryforward arising in a taxable year for which the corporation was a C corporation shall be allowed for purposes of this section as a deduction against the net recognized built-in gain of the S corporation for the taxable year. For purposes of determining the amount of any such loss which may be carried to subsequent taxable years, the amount of the net recognized built-in gain shall be treated as taxable income. Rules similar to the rules of the preceding sentences of this paragraph shall apply in the case of a capital loss carryforward arising in a taxable year for which the corporation was a C corporation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), no credit shall be allowable under part IV of subchapter A of this chapter (other than under section 34) against the tax imposed by subsection (a).

children.heading: Business credit carryforwards from C years allowed
children.label: B Business credit carryforwards from C years allowed
children.text: Notwithstanding section 1371(b)(1), any business credit carryforward under section 39 arising in a taxable year for which the corporation was a C corporation shall be allowed as a credit against the tax imposed by subsection (a) in the same manner as if it were imposed by section 11.

children.heading: Corporations which were always S corporations
children.label: 1 Corporations which were always S corporations
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to any corporation if an election under section 1362(a) has been in effect with respect to such corporation for each of its taxable years. Except as provided in regulations, an S corporation and any predecessor corporation shall be treated as 1 corporation for purposes of the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Limitation on amount of net recognized built-in gain
children.label: 2 Limitation on amount of net recognized built-in gain
A
children.text: the net unrealized built-in gain, over

children.label: B
children.text: the net recognized built-in gain for prior taxable years beginning in the recognition period.

children.label: A
children.text: the fair market value of the assets of the S corporation as of the beginning of its 1st taxable year for which an election under section 1362(a) is in effect, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate adjusted bases of such assets at such time.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount which would be the taxable income of the S corporation for such taxable year if only recognized built-in gains and recognized built-in losses were taken into account, or

children.label: ii
children.text: such corporation’s taxable income for such taxable year (determined as provided in section 1375(b)(1)(B)).

children.heading: Carryover
children.label: B Carryover
children.text: If, for any taxable year described in subparagraph (A), the amount referred to in clause (i) of subparagraph (A) exceeds the amount referred to in clause (ii) of subparagraph (A), such excess shall be treated as a recognized built-in gain in the succeeding taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: such asset was not held by the S corporation as of the beginning of the 1st taxable year for which it was an S corporation, or

children.label: i
children.text: the fair market value of such asset as of the beginning of such 1st taxable year, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the adjusted basis of the asset as of such time.

children.label: A
children.text: such asset was held by the S corporation as of the beginning of the 1st taxable year referred to in paragraph (3), and

children.label: i
children.text: the adjusted basis of such asset as of the beginning of such 1st taxable year, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the fair market value of such asset as of such time.

children.heading: Income items
children.label: A Income items
children.text: Any item of income which is properly taken into account during the recognition period but which is attributable to periods before the 1st taxable year for which the corporation was an S corporation shall be treated as a recognized built-in gain for the taxable year in which it is properly taken into account.

children.heading: Deduction items
children.label: B Deduction items
children.text: Any amount which is allowable as a deduction during the recognition period (determined without regard to any carryover) but which is attributable to periods before the 1st taxable year referred to in subparagraph (A) shall be treated as a recognized built-in loss for the taxable year for which it is allowable as a deduction.

children.heading: Adjustment to net unrealized built-in gain
children.label: C Adjustment to net unrealized built-in gain
children.text: The amount of the net unrealized built-in gain shall be properly adjusted for amounts which would be treated as recognized built-in gains or losses under this paragraph if such amounts were properly taken into account (or allowable as a deduction) during the recognition period.

children.label: A
children.text: such asset shall be treated as held by the S corporation as of the beginning of such 1st taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: any determination under paragraph (3)(B) or (4)(B) with respect to such asset shall be made by reference to the fair market value and adjusted basis of such other asset as of the beginning of such 1st taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “recognition period” means the 5-year period beginning with the 1st day of the 1st taxable year for which the corporation was an S corporation. For purposes of applying this section to any amount includible in income by reason of distributions to shareholders pursuant to section 593(e), the preceding sentence shall be applied without regard to the phrase “5-year”.

children.heading: Installment sales
children.label: B Installment sales
children.text: If an S corporation sells an asset and reports the income from the sale using the installment method under section 453, the treatment of all payments received shall be governed by the provisions of this paragraph applicable to the taxable year in which such sale was made.

children.heading: Treatment of transfer of assets from C corporation to S corporation
children.label: 8 Treatment of transfer of assets from C corporation to S corporation
i
children.text: an S corporation acquires any asset, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the S corporation’s basis in such asset is determined (in whole or in part) by reference to the basis of such asset (or any other property) in the hands of a C corporation,

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The preceding paragraphs of this subsection shall be applied by taking into account the day on which the assets were acquired by the S corporation in lieu of the beginning of the 1st taxable year for which the corporation was an S corporation.

children.heading: Subsection (c)(1) not to apply
children.label: ii Subsection (c)(1) not to apply
children.text: Subsection (c)(1) shall not apply.

children.heading: Reference to 1st taxable year
children.label: 9 Reference to 1st taxable year
children.text: Any reference in this section to the 1st taxable year for which the corporation was an S corporation shall be treated as a reference to the 1st taxable year for which the corporation was an S corporation pursuant to its most recent election under section 1362.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section including regulations providing for the appropriate treatment of successor corporations.
